sections.title: What is the Past Simple?
sections.content:
We use the **Past Simple** for things that are finished. They happened in the past.

• I **worked** yesterday.
• She **went** to London last year.
• They **saw** a film last night.

sections.title: Regular verbs
sections.content:
Most verbs add **-ed** for the past:

• work → work**ed**
• play → play**ed**
• want → want**ed**
• live → liv**ed**

sections.title: Irregular verbs
sections.content:
Some verbs do not add -ed. They have special forms:

• go → **went**
• see → **saw**
• make → **made**
• write → **wrote**

You must learn each one. See Reference → Irregular verbs.

sections.title: Negatives and Questions
sections.content:
**Negative:** I **did not** (didn't) go. She **did not** work.

**Question:** **Did** you go? **Did** she work?

With **did** we use the base form of the verb (go, work, not went, worked).

questions.question: I _____ go to work yesterday. I was ill.
